channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A24's Wall Street Roots Fuel Artistic Success with Strategic Financing
item.description: With a reputation for artistic integrity, A24 has established itself as a prominent film studio by combining edgy movies from renowned directors with strategic financing, leading to critical and commercial acclaim under modest budgets.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: iOS 18’s Vocal Actions let you swap Siri for Google Assistant or ChatGPT
item.description: While Siri is becoming more useful in iOS 18, Apple’s next big software update also includes an easier way to invoke other voice assistants instead. It’s called Vocal Actions, and it allows you to trigger custom actions with any wake phrase of your choosing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Forget drones, DJI could soon hit the trails with a shock move into e-bikes
item.description: A new DJI teaser and some social media leaks suggest the drone giant is about to make a surprise leap into e-bikes.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson) , TechRadar

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S24 FE colors just got tipped – from muted to bright
item.description: Tipster Ross Young has claimed the Samsung Galaxy S24 FE will come in black, gray, light blue, light green, and yellow.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Biden Administration Invests $504M in 12 National Technology Hubs
item.description: The Biden administration has allocated $504 million for the implementation of 12 technology hubs across Ohio, Montana, Nevada, Florida, and other locations, aiming to support innovation and economic growth.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 10 ways AI can make IT more productive
item.description: When it comes to maximizing productivity, IT leaders can turn to an array of motivators, including regular breaks, free snacks and beverages, workspace upgrades, mini contests, and so on.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI-driven exoskeleton lightens your load, elevates performance
item.description: Researchers have created an AI-powered exoskeleton that seamlessly adapts to provide remarkable mobility assistance and energy savings. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top 5 AI employee fears and how to combat them
item.description: As artificial intelligence adoption surges in business, employees are left to wonder how systems placed on “automatic” can be controlled and how long it will be before their jobs are on the chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What IT can learn from the mental health movement
item.description: While conducting C-level interviews I frequently hear comments akin to, “All this technology stuff is driving me crazy.” I know the word “crazy” is no longer an acceptable term of art in the mental health profession.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: French antitrust charges threaten Nvidia amid AI chip market surge
item.description: Nvidia is at risk of facing charges from the French antitrust authority for alleged anti-competitive behavior, a potential first in regulatory actions against the AI chip giant, according to a report by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Lenovo adds new AI solutions, expands Neptune cooling range to enable heat reuse
item.description: With the growth of artificial intelligence in data centers of all types comes not only requirements for applications and services, but the need to cool the power-hungry systems that run those workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How evolving AI regulations impact cybersecurity
item.description: While their business and tech colleagues are busy experimenting and developing new applications, cybersecurity leaders are looking for ways to anticipate and counter new, AI-driven threats. It’s always been clear that AI impacts cybersecurity, but it’s a two-way street.
item.dc:creator: Ram Movva and Aviral Verma, Info World

item.title: Hijos de Rivera aúna supercomputación e IA para investigar “los sabores del futuro”
item.description: Hijos de Rivera está desarrollando un ambicioso proyecto de investigación en el que, según relatan, por primera vez a nivel mundial se ha utilizado la computación cuántica y la inteligencia artificial (IA) para realizar una simulación de la percepción del sabor.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: UN adopts Chinese resolution with US support on closing the gap in access to artificial intelligence
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations General Assembly adopted a Chinese-sponsored resolution with U.S.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California considers unique safety regulations for AI companies, but faces tech firm opposition
item.description: California lawmakers are weighing a bill that would regulate powerful artificial intelligence systems.
item.dc:creator: Trân Nguyễn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Half a million Patelco Credit Union members locked out of credit union accounts due to security breach
item.description: Members of the credit union, which the company estimates at 500,000, were simultaneously locked out of their online accounts, barring them from checking their statement balances, sending or receiving money or changing passwords.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Martin, The Mercury News

item.title: OpenSSH vulnerability regreSSHion puts millions of servers at risk
item.description: Researchers have uncovered a serious remote code execution vulnerability in the Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH) server that could let unauthenticated attackers obtain a root shell on servers and take them over.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Eclipse Theia IDE arrives for desktop, browser
item.description: The Eclipse Foundation has announced the release of the Eclipse Theia IDE. Based on the Theia cloud and desktop IDE framework implemented in TypeScript, the Theia IDE is avialable for Windows, Linux, and macOS and can be tried online.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Stay Ahead of Voice Scams: Top 10 Threats and Proactive Measures
item.description: As AI-powered voice duplication becomes increasingly accessible, it's crucial to be aware of the latest voice scams and take immediate action to safeguard your finances. Learn how to protect yourself from these threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU finds Meta's 'pay or consent' advertising model violates tech rules
item.description: The European Union said Meta’s “pay or consent” advertising model for European users violates the bloc’s Digital Markets Act (DMA), according to preliminary findings released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The technology powering Taylor Swift, Netflix and the Sphere
item.description: Disguise is a pioneer in an exploding market for visual graphics in demand from Hollywood to the NBA.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: The Supreme Court keeps on hold efforts in Texas and Florida to regulate social media platforms
item.description: The Florida and Texas laws were signed by Republican governors in the months following decisions by Facebook and Twitter, now X, to cut then-President Donald Trump off over his posts related to the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol by his supporters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The need for Supreme Court review grew more dire when federal appeals courts reached opposite conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: DoD’s Joint Staff GenAI sprint lays out 8 internal use cases
item.description: Lt. Gen. Todd Isaacson, the CIO for the Joint Chiefs of Staff/J-6, said a review team is completing recommendations for leadership around using GenAI. The post DoD’s Joint Staff GenAI sprint lays out 8 internal use cases first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Boeing acquiring supplier Spirit AeroSystems for $4.7B
item.description: Boeing said Monday that it plans to purchase Spirit AeroSystems in a $4.7 billion all-stock deal decades after it spun off the supplier. The planemaker has for years outsourced parts of its production process to the Spirit.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: European Union accuses Facebook owner Meta of breaking digital rules with paid ad-free option
item.description: European Union regulators have accused social media company Meta Platforms of breaching the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 8 AI-powered apps that’ll actually save you time
item.description: You can’t open your eyes these days without seeing something about generative AI and all the reasons it’s, like, totally gonna revolutionize the way you work. And yet, call me surly, but most of the AI tools out there at this point seem far less impressive in practice than they do on paper.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: InfoWorld Technology of the Year Awards 2024 Nominations Now Open
item.description: The 2024 InfoWorld Technology of the Year Awards will recognize the best and most innovative products across 26 categories in software development, cloud computing, data analytics, and AI and machine learning. Nominations for the 2024 awards are now open.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Will China try to make a land grab on the moon?
item.description: “A lot of their so-called civilian space program is a military program. And I think, in effect, we are in a race,” says NASA Administrator Bill Nelson.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Too many passwords to remember? Here's what you can do
item.description: According to some unscientific studies, the average person has hundreds of passwords. That's a lot to keep track of.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Supreme Court nears the end of another momentous term. A decision on Trump's immunity looms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the last 10 days of June, on a frenetic pace of its own making, the Supreme Court touched a wide swath of American society in a torrent of decisions on abortion, guns, the environment, health, the opioid crisis, securities fraud and homelessness.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As AI gains a workplace foothold, states are trying to make sure workers don't get left behind
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — With many jobs expected to eventually rely on generative artificial intelligence, states are trying to help workers beef up their tech skills before they become outdated and get outfoxed by machines that are becoming increasingly smarter.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration
item.description: How will healthcare continue to transform? The post Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for Biden administration in a social media dispute with conservative states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday sided with the Biden administration in a dispute with Republican-led states over how far the federal government can go to combat controversial social media posts on topics including COVID-19 and election security.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A Vatican Radio territory in north Rome once blamed for electro-smog will become a solar farm
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis decreed Wednesday that an area of northern Rome, long the source of controversy because of electromagnetic waves emitted by Vatican Radio towers there, will now house a field of solar panels to fuel Vatican City. Citing the Vatican’s pledge in U.N.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Where tech, politics & giving meet: CEO Nicole Taylor considers Silicon Valley's busy intersection
item.description: Nicole Taylor has an insider's view of philanthropic trends from her seat as the president and CEO of the Silicon Valley Community Foundation.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stability AI Tries to Revive with New CEO and Investor Backing
item.description: The AI image-generator company behind Stable Diffusion, which has faced challenges, is attempting a turnaround with the appointment of a new CEO and an influx of investment from a group featuring Sean Parker, co-founder of Napster and former Facebook president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
